app.name: Review Dashboard
app.tagline: Collect, classify, and analyze reviews across channels.

common.confirm_delete: Are you sure you want to delete this?
common.name: Name

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.themes_hint: Claude reads sampled reviews of the chosen sentiment and extracts the main themes. Uses the Anthropic API — costs apply. Cached for 10 min per filter combination.
dashboard.themes_auto_hint: One mind map per sentiment band is generated and saved automatically right after each analysis. Pick a tab below; the cached map loads instantly. In auto mode you can also click an auto-category chip to scope the map further (the first new scope generates on demand and is cached).
dashboard.themes_no_reviews: No reviews match this sentiment + filters yet.
dashboard.themes_save_label_prompt: Name this mind map (so you can find it later):
dashboard.themes_count_caveat: Counts reflect each review's primary theme (a review is counted once). Sums approximate the category total; small drift is normal.
dashboard.investigations_hint: Saved (sources × categories) cards. Click one to scope every panel below.
dashboard.investigations_empty: No investigations saved yet. On the Analyze page, give your run a name to save it here.
dashboard.themes_pick_card_to_save: Select an investigation card to enable saving / loading mind maps.
dashboard.empty_state: Select an investigation card above to see its analytics. If you don't have one yet, head to the Analyze page and give your next run a name to save it as a card.
dashboard.auto_cats_hint: Click a tile to see the reviews tagged with that category.
dashboard.auto_cats_other_desc: Reviews that didn't fit any of the Top 10 categories (below confidence threshold or LLM skipped).
dashboard.themes_auto_cat_filter_hint: If none are selected, the LLM sees every category. Pick chips to restrict the sample to specific Top-10 categories before generating.

workspace.title: Workspace
workspace.confirm_reset: This will permanently delete every source, category, review, and analysis. Continue?
workspace.hint: Save your work as a .json file or load a previous one. Useful since the database is shared between visitors.

sources.title: Sources
sources.label: Label (alias)
sources.no_sources: No sources yet. Add your first source above.

categories.title: Categories
categories.name: Category name
categories.no_categories: No categories yet. Add a root category to get started.

analyze.title: Analyze
analyze.summary_language_hint: Language used for auto-category names, theme labels, and per-review summaries. Default matches your UI language.
analyze.root_scope_hint: Pick one or more root categories. The LLM will see only those subtrees and classify within them. Leave all unchecked to use the full category tree.
analyze.no_roots: No root categories defined yet. The LLM will classify against an empty tree.
analyze.source_scope_hint: Pick one or more sources whose reviews you want to analyze. Leave all unchecked to include every source.
analyze.investigation_label_hint: If you give this run a name, the (sources, categories) pair will appear as a card on the dashboard so you can click it to refilter everything later.
analyze.live_count_help: Updates automatically as you toggle mode and source checkboxes.
analyze.min_confidence_hint: When the LLM's confidence falls below this threshold, the review is stored without a category instead of being forced into the nearest leaf. Sentiment is still recorded.
analyze.classification_mode_hint: How should the LLM group reviews?
analyze.classification_mode_auto_title: Auto — derive top 10 categories from the data (default)
analyze.classification_mode_auto_hint: Reads the in-scope reviews, distils 10 distinct themes, then classifies each review into one of them. Saved on the active investigation card.
analyze.classification_mode_manual_hint: Classifies into the leaf categories you defined under Categories, restricted to the root scope you check below.
analyze.separate_user_tier_hint: The LLM also infers whether each review comes from a paid or free user from the review text (Premium / subscription mentions vs. ads / free-tier mentions). On the dashboard you'll get a Paid / Free / Unknown toggle on the auto-category tiles. This is a BETA — results can be noisy when reviews don't mention plan status.
